1.11b: Solicitor must include recommendations for appropriate documentation
1.7: Lender will not lend where the borrower and mortgagor are different entities or persons
1.14: Conveyancer may not act if the person dealing with the transaction or their immediate family is the seller
1.15: Conveyancer may not act if the person dealing with the transaction or their immediate family is the borrower
3.1.3: Lender requires notification of the seller's solicitor firm name and address
3.1.5: No other documents are acceptable for verifying identity
3.2.3: Lender requires notification of the seller's solicitor details
4.5b: Certificate of Title should be sent to completions@themortgagelender.com
4.1: Lender provides the valuation report with the instructions unless specified otherwise
5.1.1: For remortgages, customer must have owned the property for at least 6 months
6-month rule is waived for inherited property with written confirmation
Sub-sales and back-to-back transactions where seller owned for less than 6 months are not acceptable
Acceptance of sub-sales is prohibited where seller acquires title and immediately grants a lease to the borrower
Assignable contracts or irrevocable powers of attorney for intervening sellers are not acceptable
5.4.4: Lender does not require environmental or contaminated land reports
Conveyancer must report if searches identify matters impacting security
5.4.5: Personal searches are accepted if the agent has adequate professional indemnity insurance and clear Certificate of Title can be provided
5.4.6: Search insurance is not accepted for residential owner-occupied mortgages
Search insurance is accepted for Buy to Let (BTL) purchases and re-mortgages

BTL search insurance must cover Local Land Charges (LLC1), Con 29 enquiries, Water and Sewerage (Con 29DW), Coal Authority (Con 29M), commons registration, Environment Agency, mining searches, and chancel searches
5.5.3b: Original or copy of planning permission required after completion with title documents
5.5.3c: Original or copy of building regulation consents required after completion with title documents
5.5.3d: Certificates of lawful use or development required after completion with title documents
5.7.1a: Flying freeholds accepted up to 10% of floor area for residential mortgages
Flying freeholds accepted up to 15% of floor area for BTL mortgages
5.8.1: Building converted into max 4 flats where borrower occupies one and owns freehold of building is not accepted
5.8.5: Cross-over leasehold/freehold arrangements (Tyneside flats) are not accepted
5.13.1: Second charges are not permitted except for Help to Buy Equity Mortgages
5.14.15b: Indemnity insurance is not accepted for absentee or insolvent landlords; lending is prohibited in these cases
5.14.1: Residential: minimum 40 years unexpired at the end of the mortgage term
Buy to Let: minimum 50 years unexpired at the end of the mortgage term
5.14.12: Lender does not require receipts for ground rent or service charges but they must be verified by the conveyancer
5.14.13: Receipted copy of notice or evidence of service required after completion
5.15.2b: Management company share certificate is not required
5.15.2c: Signed blank stock transfer form required after completion
5.15.2d: Management company memorandum and articles of association are not required
5.17.5a: Documents signed by attorneys are not acceptable
5.20.1: Report lease issues regarding roof space solar PV panels to the Completions Team at the address on the mortgage offer
5.20.3: Solar PV panel leases must meet UK Finance minimum requirements
Lease must include a valid mortgagee break clause exercisable on possession
Solar PV provider must be responsible for removal costs and damage repair upon exercise of break clause
Building insurer must be notified of installation
5.20.4: Conveyancer must disclose Green Deal Plan details: work carried out, balance outstanding, monthly payments, term, guarantees, and EPC
6.4.4: UK Finance Disclosure of Incentives Form must be sent to completions@themortgagelender.com for new builds/renovations/conversions
Certificate of Title must not be submitted until the Disclosure of Incentives Form has been sent
6.6.1: Notify lender immediately if borrower intends to let a residential property or live in a BTL property
6.6.2: Lender does not consent to letting on owner-occupied residential mortgages
6.6.3: Tenancy agreements are not required for BTL but must comply with the Lender's Acceptable Tenancies Document
Letting is prohibited on residential owner-occupied mortgages
6.6.4: Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMO) are only accepted for BTL products specifically for HMOs
6.7.1: Accepted warranties: NHBC Buildmark, Zurich Municipal Newbuild, Premier Guarantee, Building Life Plans Scheme
Other warranties considered on a case-by-case basis via completions@themortgagelender.com
6.7.2: Complete copy of warranty documents required after completion
6.7.3: Assignments of building standards indemnity schemes must be sent
6.7.4: Professional consultant’s certificate required if no new home warranty is present
Certificate must be sent prior to exchange (purchase) or before completion (remortgage)
6.7.6: Professional consultant's certificate must be sent to the lender
6.10.2: Contact the lender for confirmation of the release recipient in each case
7.3: Residential: Deed of consent required from all occupants aged 17 or over
Buy to Let: Consent forms not required
8.1: Conveyancer may advise third parties if no conflict of interest exists
Lender's Independent Legal Advice form must be completed
9.1: Indemnity insurance accepted to protect security; recommendation must be sent before COT
Policies must cover successors in title and mortgagees
Policy should be from reputable and premium providers
Original policy should be given to borrower; copy kept on file
9.2: Minimum limit of indemnity must equal the mortgage advance
10.2b: Minimum 5 working days notice required for mortgage advance
10.3: Refer to the terms of the mortgage offer for standard deductions
10.7: Advance must be returned electronically within 24 hours of delayed completion
Include borrower name and account number with returned funds
10.10: Advance must be used on the completion date or returned within 24 hours
10.11: Interest is charged at the mortgage rate from the date funds are released
14.1.4: CH2 application not required; further advances are not permitted
14.1.5: Copy of mortgage deed and title documents required after completion; originals not required
14.2.1: Charge registration documents and delay reasons must be sent to LMS
14.2.2: Documents to upload to LMS: Priority Notice (OS1/OS2/K17), Confirmation of Application to Register (AP1 or electronic receipt), Official Copy of the Register, Notice of second charge (Help to Buy Equity Mortgages only)
16.3.7b: Standard attestation: Executed as a Deed by [Name] as attorney for and on behalf of The Mortgage Lender Limited in the presence of [Witness Signature, Name, Address]
16.3.1: Conveyancer must draft standard form of transfer/deed of covenant and send to Lender for approval
16.3.4: Transfer of equity must be sent to the Lender
16.4.1: Not applicable; letting is not permitted on residential mortgages
16.4.2: BTL: Tenancy must meet Lender's Acceptable Tenancies Document requirements
17.2.1a: Repayment remittance details are specified in the redemption statement
17.2.1b: Lender sends discharge via DS1 form
